title: podman build fails to go build, throws godump
body:
### Issue Description

for one container I tested, I am running a `podman build` and it throws an error in the stage of `go build`

I attached the go dump.
this started happening when I migrated to podman-desktop latest and moved to the new vm format.

### Steps to reproduce the issue



[go.mod.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22023709/go.mod.txt)
[go.sum.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22023814/go.sum.txt)

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. add a dockerfile to take them and run podman build
2. see a partial error

you might need to create a main.go that will use all of the packages, but I am not sure how to do that without leaking the app code



### Describe the results you received

[log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22024105/log.txt)

in here you can see the docker build steps and the error that showed up.

### Describe the results you expected

just that the podman build passes

### podman info output

tested roolful and no help

tested to reinstall all components and no help
tried to downgrade to the original version and no help

I am willing to rerun this setup to check it if needed

comments.body:
A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
what info is needed?  I had podman info before

Hello @georgettica  and thanks for the issue filing.  Unfortunately, it is hard to tell what is going on here because you didn't post the the podman build command.  Ideally you do this with `podman --log-level debug`.

Nevertheless, it appears that you are trying to build an amd64 image on arm64.  On top of that, you are also compiling go code.  Apple broke rosetta and in Podman 5.6, we had to make [some changes to use QEMU instead](https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/).  If you happen to upgrade to Tahoe, check that blog out to re-enable rosetta and see if that helps.  You could also downgrade to podman 5.5 and rosetta still works because there is an old podman-machine image.

I'm not sure there is much we can do here, but having more information would help and also confirming you meant to compile for amd64 on purpose.  Confirming the arm64 build works would be nice too.

> what info is needed? I had podman info before

sorry, i was typing out my reply after adding that label so we got out of sync a little.

actually one of my tests was to remove the flag, and I expected it to use the correct image, but I am not sure this happened.

I will add the log level to the run when I do this (just need to uninstall docker-desktop and re-add podman)

so, I have a few things

1: using
`podman machine init --image "docker://quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.5"`

as you suggested does the trick

though 
```
rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers/podman ~/.config/containers/
```

is required as you might get
```
runtime: taggedPointerPack invalid packing: ptr=0xffffa0250000 tag=0x1 packed=0xffffa02500000001 -> ptr=0xffffffffa0250000 tag=0x1
fatal error: taggedPointerPack
```

2: as seen in
```
[1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM amd64/golang:1.25-alpine AS builder
Resolving "amd64/golang" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/999-podman-machine.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/amd64/golang:1.25-alpine...
Getting image source signatures
```

changing it to

`FROM --platform=linux/arm64 golang:1.25-alpine AS builder`

solved my issue completely, maybe the combination of no

4: once the issue happens again, I will check the tahoe if I hit more issues :)


if you think this is enough to dig deeper and daignose I am happy with closing.
and if you need more feel free to ping :)
I guess I dont quite understand something.  Were you intentionally building amd64 on arm64 or not ?

yes, I was.
this was to be more compatible to the other parts of the stack

> Nevertheless, it appears that you are trying to build an amd64 image on arm64. On top of that, you are also compiling go code. Apple broke rosetta and in Podman 5.6, we had to make [some changes to use QEMU instead](https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/). If you happen to upgrade to Tahoe, check that blog out to re-enable rosetta and see if that helps.

Those instructions aren't working for me on the latest Tahoe beta (25A5351b):
matthew@MacBookPro ~ [1]> podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: unable to start "podman-machine-default": already running
matthew@MacBookPro ~ [125]> podman machine ssh "sudo touch /etc/containers/enable-rosetta"
matthew@MacBookPro ~> podman machine stop
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully
matthew@MacBookPro ~> podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
API forwarding listening on: /var/run/docker.sock
Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
matthew@MacBookPro ~> podman machine ssh "cat /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/rosetta"
cat: /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/rosetta: No such file or directory
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

So I ran the latest Tahoe version `26.0.1 (25A362)` with Rosetta, and the problem seems to be resolved.

I just upgraded today, so sorry for the slow response.

How can I help introduce Rosetta into the next release of podman/podman-desktop?
@podmanbot @baude it seems this issue is solved
